# DaVinci Resolve MCP Server — GitHub Copilot Instructions

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/agent-rules/generate.mjs — edit that, not this file. -->

This is the DaVinci Resolve MCP server: a Python MCP server (36 compound tools, 341 granular in --full mode) driving Resolve via its scripting API, plus an offline Node "advanced" server (18 tools) that authors .drp/.drt/.drx files and patches the project DB with no Resolve running. Kernel coverage: 166 guarded workflow actions.

- Canonical agent brief: AGENTS.md. Operating reference: docs/SKILL.md. Per-action depth: docs/kernels/*. Advanced (offline) catalog: resolve-advanced/README.md.
- Never modify, transcode, convert, proxy, relink, or create derivatives of source media unless explicitly asked. Analysis output goes to sidecars/scratch/the analysis project root.
- Do not duplicate version numbers, tool counts, or kernel-action counts into prose — they drift. Reference the docs above.

## Key files
- `src/server.py` — compound MCP server (36 tools); `src/resolve_mcp_server.py` — Full server (341 tools).
- `resolve-advanced/` — offline Node advanced server (18 tools). `install.py` — universal installer.

## Domain routing
Per-domain guidance lives in `.github/instructions/resolve-*.instructions.md` and,
on demand in any MCP client, as the `/<domain>_workflow` slash prompts:

- **Color / Grade** — grading, correcting, shot matching, developing looks, or applying/modifying LUTs, CDLs, DRX grades, or copied grades. Depth: docs/kernels/color-grade-kernel.md + docs/guides/color-decision-guide.md.
- **Timeline Edit** — cutting, trimming, pacing, duplicating/moving clips, copying ranges, building variants, tightening a cut, or generating an editorial changelist. Depth: docs/kernels/timeline-edit-kernel.md + docs/guides/editorial-decision-guide.md.
- **Conform / Interchange** — importing/relinking editorial, checking a conform against a reference, repairing reversed/retimed subclips, tracing grades across a re-conform, or QCing a conformed timeline. Depth: docs/kernels/timeline-conform-interchange-kernel.md.
- **Delivery / Deliverable QC** — preparing render jobs, validating render settings, QCing a finished render vs spec, building render manifests, expanding texted/textless/stems deliverables, verifying media ingest, or producing a provenance/episode report. Depth: docs/kernels/render-deliver-kernel.md.
- **Fusion Composition** — building or editing Fusion comps — titles, motion graphics, VFX, merges, masks, trackers. Depth: docs/kernels/fusion-composition-kernel.md.
- **Audio / Fairlight** — setting audio properties, syncing audio, isolating voice, generating subtitles, planning Fairlight tracks/buses, checking loudness, routing buses, or splitting/trimming/converting audio. Depth: docs/kernels/audio-fairlight-kernel.md.
- **Media Pool / Ingest** — importing media, building multicam timelines, organizing/relinking clips, normalizing metadata, or verifying/inventorying a card before ingest. Depth: docs/kernels/media-pool-ingest-kernel.md + docs/guides/multicam-setup-guide.md.
- **Auto Edit (brief → render)** — the user names source files, optional music, and the kind of video they want and expects a finished cut — autonomous talking-head/interview editing with one approval checkpoint. Depth: docs/kernels/auto-edit-kernel.md + docs/guides/editorial-decision-guide.md.
- **Media Analysis** — reading or analyzing source media (technical, visual, or transcription) to inform Resolve actions. Depth: docs/kernels/README.md + docs/guides/media-analysis-guide.md.
- **Orchestrate (ingest → deliver)** — a task spans multiple domains (edit AND grade AND deliver) and needs to survive a context reset across sessions — a full ingest-to-delivery post job. Depth: docs/kernels/orchestration-kernel.md.
- **Extension Authoring** — authoring or installing Fuse tools, DCTL/ACES LUTs, or Resolve-page scripts as generated extensions, or checking whether a new extension needs a refresh or restart. Depth: docs/kernels/extension-authoring-kernel.md.
- **Project / Database / Archive** — creating, exporting, importing, archiving, or restoring projects, switching databases, managing layout/render presets, or snapshotting project settings. Depth: docs/kernels/project-lifecycle-kernel.md.
- **Review Annotation** — adding, reading, copying, or moving markers, flags, or clip colors across a timeline, timeline item, or media pool item, or producing a read-only annotation/review report. Depth: docs/kernels/review-annotation-kernel.md.
